event: 60 years old
female
impaired consciousness
atrial fibrillation
dilated cardiomyopathy
receiving oral warfarin
implantation of a biventricular pacing implantable cardioverter defibrillator
found lying at home
transported to our hospital by ambulance
Japan Coma Scale (JCS) score was II-10
Glasgow Coma Scale (GCS) score was 14 (E3V5M6)
no clear neurological deficits
non-contrast head CT revealed hemorrhage in the third and fourth ventricles, and bilateral lateral ventricles with left dominance
hemorrhage was confined within the ventricles
brain 3D-CTA was performed
source of bleeding was unclear in the arterial phase
spot enhancement on the lateral wall of the anterior horn of the left lateral ventricle in the venous phase
ventricular drainage was not performed
blood pressure control was initiated in the intensive care unit
cerebral angiograph revealed an aneurysm (3.8 mm in size) at the distal site of the mLSA arising from the proximal A1 segment of the left anterior cerebral artery (ACA)
considered to be a ruptured aneurysm
aneurysm embolization was performed
under general anesthesia
right femoral introducer was replaced by a 4Fr 80-cm ASAHI FUBUKI Dilator Kit
systemic heparinization
guiding sheath was navigated into the petrous portion of the left internal carotid artery
4.2Fr ASAHI FUBUKI was used as a distal access catheter
Mirage guide wire, a Marathon catheter was advanced to the left A1
mLSA arising in a direction reverse to the path of the left A1
compression study of the right common carotid artery confirmed the anterior communicating artery (AComA)
guiding sheath was introduced into the petrous portion of the right internal carotid artery
Marathon was advanced through the AComA into the mLSA origin
unable to be advanced to a site near the aneurysm
changed to a combination of a DeFrictor nano catheter and TENROU S10 guide wire
mLSA cannulation was possible
selective angiography from the DeFrictor confirmed the location of the catheter tip proximal to the aneurysm
33% NBCA-Lipiodol mixture was slowly injected from the DeFrictor
perforating artery distal and proximal to the aneurysm were confirmed to be filled with NBCA
DeFrictor was removed
angiography after NBCA injection demonstrated disappearance of the aneurysm
no signs of hemorrhagic complications or cerebral infarction on the postoperative head CT
no impaired consciousness or paralysis developed
Lipiodol remained as a high-density spot on the lateral ventricular wall of the anterior horn of the left lateral ventricle
subsequently, sepsis triggered by pneumonia developed
a decrease in muscle strength associated with disuse remained
after 6-month rehabilitation, she was discharged to home with a modified Rankin Scale (mRS) of 1
